task_completion.description: Navigate to Emoncms and log in as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser address bar shows localhost or 127.0.0.1, and the agent successfully logs into the Emoncms dashboard using the admin credentials.
The Emoncms 'Feeds' page is visible, typically accessed via the main navigation menu.

task_completion.description: Locate the 'Test Feed' in the feeds list
task_completion.visual_evidence: A feed with the name 'Test Feed' is visible in the list of feeds on the screen.

task_completion.description: Initiate and confirm the deletion of 'Test Feed'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the delete button (often a trash can icon) next to 'Test Feed' and accepts any resulting confirmation dialog or modal.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'Test Feed' has been removed
task_completion.visual_evidence: The feeds list is displayed again, and 'Test Feed' is no longer present in the list.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to interact with Emoncms
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser interface being used to navigate the application, rather than terminal commands like curl.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal database commands to delete the feed
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open showing direct SQL (MariaDB) or Redis commands being executed to bypass the web UI.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Emoncms application
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual interface matches the standard Emoncms web dashboard, not a text editor or a mocked HTML file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must log into the Emoncms web interface at http://localhost using the credentials admin/admin. The target feed to be deleted is explicitly named 'Test Feed'.
